name: Hostinger
description: Budget-friendly traditional web hosting provider with shared, cloud, and VPS plans that can serve static sites, plus a newer Node.js web apps hosting feature for JavaScript framework deployments

description.value: Budget-friendly traditional web hosting provider with shared, cloud, and VPS plans that can serve static sites, plus a newer Node.js web apps hosting feature for JavaScript framework deployments
description.comment: Hostinger is primarily a traditional shared/cloud hosting provider, not a purpose-built static site hosting platform like Netlify or Vercel. You can host static HTML/CSS/JS files on any Hostinger shared hosting plan by uploading them to public_html. In late 2025, Hostinger launched a Node.js web apps hosting feature (available on Business and Cloud plans) that supports deploying React, Vue, Angular, and other JS framework apps with GitHub integration and auto-builds. However, this is still fundamentally different from a modern JAMstack/static hosting platform with edge-first CDN delivery, deploy previews, and serverless functions.

parent-company.comment: Founded in 2004 as Hosting Media in Kaunas, Lithuania. Rebranded to Hostinger in 2011. Independently operated; employee-owned structure. In 2021, private equity firm ConHostinger acquired an approximately 31% controlling stake. Parent company of 000webhost, Niagahoster, and Zyro.

year-launched.comment: Originally founded as Hosting Media in 2004 in Kaunas, Lithuania. Rebranded to Hostinger in 2011. The Node.js web apps hosting feature launched in November 2025, but the core shared hosting service has been available since 2004.

open-source.comment: The core platform is proprietary. Hostinger has a GitHub organization with 108 repositories (mostly infrastructure tools and Ansible roles), but the hosting platform itself is not open source.

primary-focus.comment: Hostinger's primary focus is general-purpose web hosting (shared, cloud, VPS), not static sites or JAMstack specifically. Static file hosting is a basic capability of any shared hosting plan. The newer Node.js web apps hosting feature adds some modern deployment capabilities but doesn't fundamentally change the platform's positioning as a traditional host. Listed as 'static-sites' because that is the narrowest applicable tag, but this is a stretch -- Hostinger is not a static-site-first platform.

free-tier.comment: Hostinger does not offer a free hosting tier. All plans require payment. Their subsidiary 000webhost offers free hosting, but that is a separate product. Hostinger offers a 30-day money-back guarantee, which is a trial period, not a permanent free tier.

free-tier-bandwidth.comment: No free tier exists. The cheapest plan (Web Single at ~$1.99/month promotional) includes 100 GB bandwidth.

free-tier-build-minutes.comment: No free tier exists. Hostinger does not use a build-minutes model; traditional shared hosting does not have a build pipeline concept. The newer Node.js web apps hosting has a build system but no documented build-minutes limit.

paid-plan-starting-price.comment: Premium Web Hosting starts at ~$2.99/month on a 48-month commitment (promotional rate). Supports up to 3 websites with 20 GB SSD storage and unlimited bandwidth. Renewal price is significantly higher (~$7.99/month). The Business plan ($3.99/month promotional) adds Node.js web apps hosting, CDN, and more storage. Note: Node.js web apps hosting requires the Business plan or higher.

bandwidth-overage-cost.comment: Most Hostinger plans (Premium and above) include unlimited bandwidth. The Web Single plan has a 100 GB limit. Hostinger does not document overage charges; they likely throttle or suspend sites that exceed fair-use limits. Inode limits (200,000-600,000 files) act as an indirect capacity constraint.

enterprise-pricing.comment: Hostinger offers Cloud Enterprise and Cloud Enterprise Plus plans, as well as Agency hosting tiers with higher resource limits. Custom enterprise pricing is available for large deployments.

git-integration.comment: Hostinger supports Git deployment via hPanel for shared hosting (any public Git repository URL with webhook-based continuous deployment). The newer Node.js web apps hosting feature has direct GitHub integration with OAuth-based repository selection and automatic redeployment on commits. GitLab and Bitbucket repositories can be used via manual Git URL on shared hosting, but the Node.js web apps feature currently supports GitHub only.

auto-deploy.comment: On shared hosting, continuous deployment is available via webhooks -- pushes to a connected branch trigger automatic file pulls. On Node.js web apps hosting, GitHub commits automatically trigger rebuilds and redeployment.

cli-deployment.comment: Hostinger does not offer a dedicated CLI tool for deployment. Files can be uploaded via FTP/SFTP, File Manager, or Git, but there is no Hostinger-specific CLI comparable to Vercel CLI or Netlify CLI. Deployment from IDE (VS Code, Cursor) is mentioned for Node.js web apps hosting, but this is not a traditional CLI deployment tool.

build-system.comment: The Node.js web apps hosting feature (Business plan and above) includes a built-in build pipeline that auto-detects frameworks, runs build commands, and deploys the output. Traditional shared hosting does NOT have a build system -- you upload pre-built files. The build system is only available for Node.js web apps, not for general static file hosting.

supported-frameworks.comment: The Node.js web apps hosting feature has auto-detection for: Next.js, Express.js, React, React Router, Vue.js, Angular, Vite, Parcel, and Preact. Additional framework support is planned. Note: on traditional shared hosting, any static site generator output can be uploaded manually, but there is no auto-detection or zero-config support.

monorepo-support.comment: Not documented. Hostinger's Node.js web apps hosting may support specifying a subdirectory, but monorepo support is not explicitly mentioned in their documentation.

deploy-previews.comment: Hostinger does not offer deploy previews or preview deployments for pull requests. There is no equivalent to Netlify Deploy Previews or Vercel Preview Deployments.

instant-rollbacks.comment: Hostinger offers backup restoration (7 most recent backup dates available), but this is a traditional backup restore process that takes 10-15 minutes, not instant rollback to a previous deployment. There is no concept of promoting a previous deployment artifact to production without rebuilding.

deploy-notifications.comment: Hostinger sends email notifications for various account events. There are no documented Slack, webhook, or GitHub status integrations for deployment notifications. The platform lacks the deployment-centric notification infrastructure of modern hosting platforms.

global-cdn.comment: Hostinger uses Cloudflare CDN for serving static assets (CSS, JS, images) on all plans. Additionally, Hostinger has launched its own in-house CDN (available on Business plans and above) with edge servers in Europe, Asia, North America, South America, South Africa, and Australia. The Node.js web apps hosting also includes CDN. Note: the origin servers are in specific data centers, not edge-distributed like Cloudflare Pages or Vercel.

cdn-provider.comment: Static assets are served via Cloudflare's CDN network. Hostinger also offers its own in-house CDN (beta, Business plans+). Origin servers are hosted in Hostinger's own data centers across multiple regions.

edge-locations.value: 10+ data centers; CDN via Cloudflare (330+ locations)
edge-locations.comment: Hostinger operates data centers in the US, UK, France, Netherlands, Lithuania, India, Singapore, Indonesia, Brazil, and Malaysia (launched July 2025). Static asset delivery through Cloudflare CDN benefits from Cloudflare's 330+ edge locations, but origin requests are served from a single data center per site.

http2.comment: Hostinger uses LiteSpeed Web Server, which supports HTTP/2 by default for all HTTPS connections. HTTP/2 is enabled automatically on all Hostinger hosting plans.

http3.comment: LiteSpeed Web Server supports HTTP/3 (QUIC) natively and was the first web server to offer production-grade HTTP/3 support. Since Hostinger uses LiteSpeed, HTTP/3 should be available, though Hostinger does not prominently document this feature themselves.

response-headers.comment: Custom response headers can be configured via .htaccess files on shared hosting plans. This requires manual configuration rather than a dashboard-based interface. The Hostinger CDN also allows some header configuration.

image-optimization.comment: Hostinger does not offer built-in image optimization or transformation as a hosting feature. The Hostinger CDN includes basic image compression, but there is no equivalent to Vercel Image Optimization or Cloudflare Image Resizing. Users must optimize images at build time or use external services.

asset-compression.comment: Gzip compression is supported on all Hostinger Web and Cloud hosting plans via mod_gzip and mod_deflate modules. Brotli compression may be available through Cloudflare CDN when enabled, but is not natively documented as a Hostinger feature. LiteSpeed Web Server supports Brotli, but Hostinger's documentation only explicitly mentions gzip.

custom-domains.comment: Custom domains are fully supported on all plans. Hostinger also offers domain registration services and includes a free domain for the first year on annual plans. Multiple domains can be hosted depending on the plan (1 on Single, 3 on Premium, 50-300 on higher plans).

free-ssl.comment: Free SSL certificates are included with all hosting plans for all domains and subdomains. SSL is automatically installed when a domain is added, a subdomain is created, or a domain is parked. Domain Validation (DV) type certificates.

wildcard-ssl.comment: Wildcard SSL certificates are NOT supported on Web or Cloud hosting plans. Wildcard SSL is only supported on VPS hosting plans. The free SSL provided on shared/cloud hosting covers individual domains and subdomains but not wildcard certificates.

auto-https-redirect.comment: HTTPS redirect can be configured through hPanel's SSL settings or via .htaccess rules. Hostinger enables HTTPS by default when SSL is installed, though the automatic redirect configuration may require manual setup depending on the setup method.

custom-domain-preview.comment: Hostinger does not offer deploy previews, so custom domains per preview are not applicable.

serverless-functions.comment: Hostinger does not offer serverless functions as a built-in, first-party feature. The platform uses traditional server-based hosting (LiteSpeed/Apache on shared, containerized for Node.js apps). There are no Lambda-like or edge function capabilities.

functions-runtime.comment: While Hostinger does not have serverless functions, the Node.js web apps hosting supports Node.js applications. Traditional shared hosting supports PHP natively. VPS hosting supports any runtime. These are persistent server processes, not on-demand functions.

edge-functions.comment: Hostinger does not offer edge functions. All compute runs in specific data center regions, not at the edge.

scheduled-functions.comment: Cron jobs are available on all shared and cloud hosting plans via hPanel. These are traditional server-side cron jobs, not serverless scheduled functions, but they serve a similar purpose for running tasks on a schedule.

form-handling.comment: Hostinger does not offer built-in form handling as a hosting feature. Static sites would need to use PHP scripts on shared hosting or external form services. The Website Builder product has its own form handling, but that is a separate product from the hosting platform.

authentication.comment: Hostinger does not provide built-in user authentication for hosted sites. No identity/auth service is offered. Users must implement their own authentication or use third-party services.

kv-storage.comment: Hostinger does not offer a built-in key-value or edge storage solution. Redis/Memcached may be available on VPS plans but not as a managed first-party service on shared/cloud hosting.

database-integration.comment: MySQL databases are included with all shared and cloud hosting plans (2-300 databases depending on plan tier). This is traditional MySQL database hosting, not a modern managed database service, but it is a first-party integrated feature.

redirects.comment: URL redirects (301, 302) can be configured through hPanel's Redirect tool or via .htaccess files. Both methods are well-documented.

rewrites.comment: URL rewrites are supported via .htaccess mod_rewrite rules on Apache/LiteSpeed. This requires manual configuration but is a standard feature of the underlying web server.

custom-404.comment: Custom 404 error pages can be configured via .htaccess (ErrorDocument 404) or by placing a 404.html file in the appropriate directory. Standard Apache/LiteSpeed functionality.

spa-fallback.comment: SPA fallback routing can be configured via .htaccess rewrite rules that direct all non-file requests to index.html. This is a manual configuration, not an automatic feature, but it works with frameworks like React Router and Vue Router.

headers-config.comment: Custom response headers can be set via .htaccess files (Header set/append directives). This includes security headers, caching headers, CORS headers, etc. Requires manual .htaccess configuration.

ab-testing.comment: Hostinger does not offer built-in A/B testing or traffic splitting capabilities. No CDN-level split testing or deployment-based traffic distribution is available.

free-team-members.comment: No free tier exists. Hostinger's Account Sharing feature allows granting access to other users, but this is tied to the paid hosting account. The number of collaborators that can be granted access is not clearly documented. There is no team plan with a specific number of free seats.

rbac.comment: Basic role-based access control is available through Account Sharing. Users with granted access have a restricted view of the control panel and cannot manage payment methods, invite others, view profile information, perform domain management, delete accounts, set up new plans, or claim free offerings. However, this is more limited than enterprise RBAC systems with granular per-resource permissions.

audit-logs.comment: Hostinger does not offer audit logs as a documented feature on shared or cloud hosting plans. There is no audit trail of user actions visible to account owners.

environment-variables.comment: Environment variables can be configured for Node.js web apps hosting through hPanel. Traditional shared hosting supports environment variables via .htaccess (SetEnv) or PHP configuration, but not through a dedicated UI for build-time variables.

preview-comments.comment: Hostinger does not offer deploy previews, and therefore has no preview commenting feature.

ddos-protection.comment: DDoS protection is included on all hosting plans. Hostinger uses a multi-layered approach including real-time monitoring, automatic attack detection, traffic filtering, and remotely triggered black hole (RTBH) routing. The Node.js web apps hosting also includes DDoS protection.

waf.comment: Hostinger employs a Web Application Firewall as part of its security infrastructure, using mod_security and other security modules. The Node.js web apps hosting also includes WAF protection. The WAF is platform-managed, not user-configurable.

soc2.comment: Hostinger does not appear to have SOC 2 certification. Their documented compliance certifications include ISO/IEC 27001:2017, but SOC 2 is not mentioned in their security documentation.

gdpr.comment: Hostinger documents GDPR compliance. Being based in Lithuania (EU member state), they follow EU data protection regulations. Their security documentation explicitly lists GDPR among applicable laws and regulations.

password-protection.comment: Hostinger offers a Password Protect Directories feature through hPanel that allows password-protecting websites or specific directories. Can also be configured manually via .htaccess/.htpasswd files.

ip-access-control.comment: IP-based access control is available through multiple methods: hPanel's IP Manager, .htaccess rules, and Hostinger CDN's traffic blocking feature. Supports blocking individual IPs, IP ranges, and country-based blocking.

documentation-quality.comment: Hostinger has a comprehensive Help Center and extensive tutorials covering a wide range of topics. Documentation is generally clear and well-organized for traditional hosting tasks. However, documentation for the newer Node.js web apps hosting feature is still maturing. The tutorials section is strong for beginners but lacks the developer-focused depth of platforms like Vercel or Netlify docs. Search functionality and categorization are adequate.

integrations.comment: Hostinger supports Google Analytics, Hotjar, and Meta Pixel integration. WordPress and WooCommerce hosting are first-party offerings. Auto-installable CMS options include WordPress, PrestaShop, OpenCart, and others. Built-in ecommerce through Website Builder. However, there is no formal integration marketplace, and the integrations are more about platform compatibility than purpose-built connectors.

community.comment: Hostinger has a Discord community (primarily focused on Hostinger Horizons/website builder). Community forums exist for peer support. Stack Overflow has some Hostinger-related questions, though there is no dedicated Hostinger tag with high activity. No GitHub Discussions.

status-page.comment: Public status page at statuspage.hostinger.com tracking core services, hosting infrastructure, data centers, website builders, and email services. Shows uptime history over 90 days with email/SMS subscription for updates.
